description:
Your role: act as an integration-developer who needs to do a quick smoke-test against an
    already populated SQLite database.

    Starting point
    1. There is a ready-to-use database file at
       /home/user/dev_data/api_responses.db
       (the directory and file already exist).
    2. The database contains a table named responses with the exact schema
       (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, endpoint TEXT, status_code INTEGER, ts TEXT).

    Goal
    Produce a *single* terminal command that
    • connects to the database with the sqlite3 CLI,  
    • counts how many rows in the responses table belong to the endpoint
      "/v1/accounts" and have status_code = 200,  
    • writes the result to a new text file at
      /home/user/api_status_summary.txt.

    Required output format in api_status_summary.txt
    • The file must contain exactly one line.  
    • That line must have the literal key SUCCESS_COUNT followed by an equals
      sign and the integer count, then a single newline.
      Example (don’t infer the number from this example):
          SUCCESS_COUNT=12⏎
      There must be **no** extra spaces, headers, quotes, or additional lines.

    Hints & constraints
    • You can accomplish everything with a single sqlite3 invocation that
      redirects its output to the target file.  
    • Do not create temporary files.  
    • The agent is limited to normal user permissions; the target path is
      writable.  
    • The automated checker will verify that:
        - /home/user/api_status_summary.txt exists,
        - its permissions are 0644 or more restrictive for group/other,
        - it contains exactly one line in the format described above and with
          the correct count value.

    Once the command has run successfully, no further steps are necessary.
truth:
Expected file created by agent
    Path: /home/user/api_status_summary.txt
    Permissions: 0644 (rw-r--r--)
    Contents (with a trailing newline):
        SUCCESS_COUNT=2\n

    Pre-existing resources prepared by the test harness
    1. Directory: /home/user/dev_data
    2. Database:  /home/user/dev_data/api_responses.db
       • SQLite schema:
         CREATE TABLE responses(
             id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
             endpoint TEXT,
             status_code INTEGER,
             ts TEXT
         );
       • Rows:
           1 | /v1/accounts | 200 | 2023-10-01T10:00:00Z
           2 | /v1/accounts | 500 | 2023-10-01T10:01:00Z
           3 | /v1/accounts | 200 | 2023-10-01T10:02:00Z
           4 | /v1/payments | 200 | 2023-10-01T10:03:00Z
